entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q4 2024 edition of our "What's New "blog series. SAP Build is a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This quarter, we're excited to highlight many new releases, including AI-driven features that transform your ability to automate and innovate. Let's dive in!

Below, you will find product-specific highlights for:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Code
  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Process Automation

Create and Manage Process Variants

SAP Build Process Automation now allows you to create and manage process variants. With pre-defined process steps and guardrails, process experts and professional developers can model different variants for different user groups using a configuration-only experience. By defining variant conditions and actions, workflows can be adapted to your specific business needs. With this feature, you can manage tailored workflows for various scenarios without impacting the overall process template. Learn how to create and manage process variants in the SAP Help Portal.

Consume RFC / BAPI in a Process via Actions

To help you extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise solution, you can now create Actions based on RFCs / BAPIs. Utilizing the BAPI node library, you can directly incorporate RFC actions, streamlining the integration process. Easily search and browse RFC actions to locate the necessary functions for your workflows, maintain system integrity, and reduce complexity.

 

SAP Build Code

Generative AI Capabilities in the Mobile Development Kit

Enhanced generative AI capabilities for the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) can now help you simplify your app creation process. Developers can:

  • Generate Application Logic: Automatically produce metadata-driven logic, reducing manual coding efforts.
  • Create Forms and Workflows: Quickly develop forms and workflows with AI assistance, accelerating development.
  • Build Robust Mobile Solutions: Leverage AI to minimize manual effort, allowing focus on delivering impactful solutions.

Learn more in this blog by @BirgitS. 

SAPUI5 Development with TypeScript and UI5 Linter

AI-Driven Migration to TypeScript with Joule: Joule, the AI assistant integrated into SAP Build Code, now enables a seamless migration of SAPUI5 applications from JavaScript to TypeScript. TypeScript offers enhanced development efficiency, providing a structured and scalable approach to building SAPUI5 apps. Learn more about this powerful capability in this blog by @martinhaeuser.

Release of UI5 Linter v1.0: SAP Build Code introduces UI5 Linter v1.0, a tool designed to ensure code quality and alignment with best practices. This release empowers developers to write cleaner, more maintainable SAPUI5 code, enhancing the overall development lifecycle.

A New Experience for Extending SAP Fiori Apps

Adaptation Editor

Adaptation Editor for SAPUI5 Projects: SAP Build Code now features the Adaptation Editor, a new tool for extending SAPUI5 applications. Replacing the SAPUI5 Visual Editor, the Adaptation Editor is available in SAP Business Application Studio, offering an intuitive and consistent experience across environments. Developers can leverage this updated tool for streamlined SAPUI5 extensions with improved performance. For details, see the announcement blog by @OliverGraeff. 

Adaptation vs. Extension Projects: SAPUI5 flexibility supports both adaptation and extension projects, enabling tailored app modifications for developers, key users, and end users. Adaptation projects, now the strategic approach for extending SAPUI5 apps, provide advanced tools for code-based customization. To learn about the differences and benefits of these project types, explore this comparison blog.

SAP Build Apps

Additional HTTP Request Headers for BTP Destinations

Gain greater control and flexibility when integrating external services with SAP Build Apps. You can now configure additional HTTP request headers for SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) destinations, enhancing integration security and customization. The new flow function allows you to upload binary files using multipart/form-data as request body type. To learn more, see HTTP Destination Request flow function reference documentation.

A New Top Navigation Bar

SAP Build Apps now has an updated toolbar and navigation menu for a more intuitive experience. Frequently used features like Variables are now accessible directly from the toolbar. Tabs like "UI Canvas" and "Data" have been renamed to "User Interface" and "Integrations." At the same time, less-used options such as Theme and Navigation are grouped under "App Settings" to declutter the interface. Actions like Preview and Publish are prominently placed in the upper-right corner, simplifying release versioning and deployment. For more details, read the feature review blog post by @Dan_Wroblewski.

SAP Build Work Zone

Integration with Joule for Work Zone Standard Edition

Joule is now integrated into SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition, enhancing the workspace with AI-powered capabilities. This integration delivers intelligent workspace recommendations to boost efficiency, proactive insights tailored to tasks and priorities, and streamlined workflows to improve collaboration and focus. For more examples of how Joule can transform your workspaces, check out @Yumnahabbasi's blog post.

Guided Process Wizard

The Guided Process Wizard combines SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation to simplify creating structured, step-by-step workflows. With this integration, you can design guided processes in Process Automation and deploy them as wizards in Work Zone. Build customizable workflows with dynamic UI elements like cards and widgets to streamline complex onboarding and invoice processing tasks. Guided experiences are included in the SAP Build Work Zone Advanced Edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. Read more in the FAQ blogpost by @Yumnahabbasi.

Learn More

For a complete list of new features, check out the following resources:

  • What's New Viewer
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Road Map Explorer

To stay updated with the latest announcements and innovations, follow the SAP Builders Community and converse with fellow SAP Builders.

We look forward to seeing how you leverage these new capabilities to achieve your goals!

entry.title: SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 4 季度版本
entry.content:
更快、更智能、更高效地构建

欢迎阅读我们2024年第四季度的“产品新功能”博客系列。本文为翻译文，原文链接What’s New in SAP Build – Q4 2024 Edition，原文作者JesseSukhbayar。

SAP Build 是一款业务应用程序开发和自动化解决方案，提供了一套全面的低代码、专业代码和生成式 AI 工具。本季度，我们很高兴为大家介绍许多新版本，其中包括 AI 驱动的功能，这些功能将彻底改变您的自动化和创新能力。让我们一起来看看吧！

以下内容将为您介绍以下产品的具体亮点：

  • SAP Build Process Automation

  • SAP Build Code

  • SAP Build Apps

  • SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build 流程自动化

创建和管理流程变体

SAP Build 流程自动化现在支持创建和管理流程变体。通过预定义的流程步骤和防护机制，流程专家和专业开发人员可以使用纯配置的方式为不同的用户群体建模不同的流程变体。通过定义变体条件和操作，工作流可以根据您的具体业务需求进行调整。借助此功能，您可以为各种场景管理定制化的工作流，而不会影响整体流程模板。

您可以在 SAP 帮助门户 中了解如何创建和管理流程变体。

在流程中通过 Actions 使用 RFC / BAPI

为了帮助您扩展 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 私有版和本地部署解决方案，您现在可以基于 RFC / BAPI 创建 Actions。通过利用 BAPI 节点库，您可以直接集成 RFC Actions，从而简化集成流程。您可以轻松搜索和浏览 RFC Actions，以找到工作流所需的必要功能，同时维护系统完整性并降低复杂性。

这一功能使您能够更高效地将 SAP S/4HANA 的功能集成到自动化流程中，从而提升业务操作的灵活性和效率。

SAP Build Code

移动开发工具包（MDK）中的生成式 AI 功能增强

移动开发工具包（MDK）的生成式 AI 功能现已增强，可以帮助您简化应用程序创建过程。开发人员可以：

  1. 生成应用程序逻辑：自动生成基于元数据的逻辑，减少手动编码工作量。

  2. 创建表单和工作流：在 AI 的协助下快速开发表单和工作流，加速开发进程。

  3. 构建强大的移动解决方案：利用 AI 减少手动操作，专注于交付具有影响力的解决方案。

通过这些功能，开发人员可以更高效地构建移动应用，同时将更多精力投入到业务价值的实现中。

了解更多详情，请阅读 @BirgitS 的博客 。

SAPUI5 开发：TypeScript 和 UI5 Linter

使用 Joule 进行 AI 驱动的 TypeScript 迁移

Joule 是集成到 SAP Build Code 中的 AI 助手，现在支持将 SAPUI5 应用程序从 JavaScript 无缝迁移到 TypeScript。TypeScript 提供了更高的开发效率，为构建 SAPUI5 应用程序提供了一种结构化且可扩展的方法。了解更多关于这一强大功能的信息，请阅读 @martinhaeuser 的博客 。

UI5 Linter v1.0 发布

SAP Build Code 推出了 UI5 Linter v1.0，这是一款旨在确保代码质量并与最佳实践保持一致的工具。此版本使开发人员能够编写更清晰、更易维护的 SAPUI5 代码，从而提升整体开发生命周期的效率。

通过这些新功能，开发人员可以更高效地构建高质量的 SAPUI5 应用程序，同时减少错误并提高代码的可维护性。

扩展 SAP Fiori 应用程序的全新体验

适用于 SAPUI5 项目的 Adaptation Editor

SAP Build Code 现在推出了 Adaptation Editor，这是一款用于扩展 SAPUI5 应用程序的新工具。Adaptation Editor 取代了 SAPUI5 Visual Editor，并在 SAP Business Application Studio 中提供，为跨环境开发提供了直观且一致的体验。开发人员可以利用这一更新后的工具，以更高的性能简化 SAPUI5 扩展。详情请参阅 @OliverGraeff 的博客 。

适应项目与扩展项目

SAPUI5 的灵活性支持适应项目（Adaptation Projects）和扩展项目（Extension Projects），使开发人员、关键用户和最终用户能够对应用程序进行定制化修改。适应项目现已成为扩展 SAPUI5 应用程序的战略性方法，提供了基于代码定制的高级工具。要了解这些项目类型的区别和优势，请参阅 这篇博客 。

通过这些新工具和方法，开发人员可以更灵活、高效地扩展和定制 SAPUI5 应用程序，满足多样化的业务需求。

SAP Build Apps

为 BTP 目标服务添加额外的 HTTP 请求

在将外部服务与 SAP Build Apps 集成时，您现在可以配置额外的 HTTP 请求头，从而获得更高的控制性和灵活性。这一功能增强了集成安全性和定制化能力。此外，新的流函数（flow function）支持使用 multipart/form-data 作为请求体类型上传二进制文件。

要了解更多详细信息，请参阅 HTTP Destination Request 流函数参考文档。

通过这些更新，开发人员可以更安全、更灵活地集成外部服务，并支持更复杂的文件上传场景。

全新的顶部导航栏

SAP Build Apps 现在提供了更新的工具栏和导航菜单，为用户带来更直观的体验。常用的功能（如“变量”）现在可以直接从工具栏访问。同时，部分标签名称进行了更新，例如：

  • “UI Canvas” 更名为 “用户界面”

  • “Data” 更名为 “集成”

此外，使用频率较低的选项（如“主题”和“导航”）被归类到 “应用设置” 中，以减少界面杂乱感。常用操作（如“预览”和“发布”）则被显著地放置在右上角，简化了版本管理和部署流程。

要了解更多详细信息，请阅读 @Dan_Wroblewski 的功能评测博客 。

通过这些改进，SAP Build Apps 的用户界面更加简洁高效，帮助开发人员更专注于应用程序的设计和开发。

SAP Build Work Zone

Joule 与 Work Zone 标准版的集成

Joule 现已集成到 SAP Build Work Zone 标准版 中，为工作区提供了 AI 驱动的强大功能。这一集成带来了以下优势：

  • 智能工作区推荐：提升工作效率。

  • 主动洞察：根据任务和优先级提供定制化建议。

  • 简化的工作流：改善协作并帮助用户更专注于关键任务。

通过 Joule 的 AI 能力，用户可以更高效地管理工作区，并获得个性化的支持。

要了解更多关于 Joule 如何改变您的工作区的示例，请阅读 @Yumnahabbasi 的博客 。

引导式流程向导

引导式流程向导（Guided Process Wizard）结合了 SAP Build Work Zone 和 SAP Build 流程自动化，简化了创建结构化、分步工作流的过程。通过这一集成，您可以在流程自动化中设计引导式流程，并将其作为向导部署到 Work Zone 中。

您可以利用动态 UI 元素（如卡片和小部件）构建可定制的工作流，从而简化复杂的入职和发票处理等任务。引导式体验功能包含在 SAP Build Work Zone 高级版 和 SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone 中。

要了解更多详细信息，请阅读 @Yumnahabbasi 的 FAQ 博客。

 

了解更多

如需查看完整的新功能列表，请访问以下资源：

  • What's New Viewer（新功能查看器）

  • SAP 帮助门户

  • SAP Road Map Explorer（ SAP 路线图探索器）

为了及时了解最新公告和创新动态，请关注 SAP Builders Community，并与社区中的其他 SAP Builders 交流互动。

entry.title: The secret to better "looking" tasks in SAP Build Process Automation: Customizing My Inbox
entry.content:
When working with workflow applications, having access to the right information at the right time is critical. The My Inbox app in SAP Build Process Automation is a key tool for managing and interacting with workflow tasks.

While many are familiar with adding custom parameters in classic workflow scenarios using SAP Business Application Studio, fewer know that similar enhancements are possible in SAP Build Process Automation. 

 

Yes, you can add custom attributes to tasks in the My Inbox app in SAP Build Process Automation. This blog will guide you through the steps to achieve that, covering everything from syntax to UI customization. But before diving into the technical steps, let’s take a moment to understand the business value of this enhancement. 

Business Value of Adding Custom Attributes

Customizing the My Inbox app to display additional attributes isn’t just about improving the appearance—it brings significant business benefits: 

Faster Task Identification: 

By showing key information directly in the task list, users can quickly identify the task they need without having to open each one individually. This reduces time spent searching through tasks and increases productivity. 

Enhanced Search Capabilities: 

When custom attributes are added, they become searchable through the My Inbox app’s search box. This means users can find specific tasks based on those attributes without needing to manually sift through task details. Without custom attributes, only standard fields are searchable, limiting the ability to find tasks efficiently. Additionally, if you turn on My Inbox Expert view, those attributes will appear as columns. 

Better User Experience: 

Providing more information at a glance makes the My Inbox app more intuitive and user-friendly. Users feel more in control of their tasks, leading to a smoother workflow experience and improved satisfaction :-).

Step 1: Adding Custom Attributes in the Standard Form

To display additional attributes in the My Inbox app, you first need to define them within your form. This requires the correct syntax for adding custom parameters. This is also described in documentation.

  1. Open your project in SAP Build Process Automation. 

  2. Navigate to the form associated with your task. 

  3. Add the custom attributes following bellow syntax.

     

     

Step 2: Creating a Local Copy of the My Inbox App in SAP Build Work Zone 

Once your Build project is updated to include custom attributes, the next step is to enable their display in the My Inbox app. 

  1. Access the SAP Build Work Zone. 

  2. Create a local copy of the standard My Inbox app by cloning it.

  3. Edit the configuration of your local My Inbox app. Look for the parameter showAdditionalAttributes in the app settings.

  4. Set the parameter showAdditionalAttributes to true to allow custom attributes to be displayed.   

     

Step 3: Verifying the Custom Attributes Display 

  1. Open a task in the app.

  2. You should now see the additional custom attributes displayed alongside standard task details. 

     

If everything is configured correctly, the My Inbox app will now display your custom parameters, making it easier for users to access all necessary information at a glance.   

Continual Improvements in SAP Build Process Automation 

We’re glad to see that SAP is continually adding options previously available in Business Application Studio into SAP Build Process Automation. This ensures a smooth transition for developers familiar with the classic environment while introducing new possibilities for enhanced workflow customization.

We look forward to seeing even more features and capabilities added in future updates!

entry.title: NexGen AI Agents for SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
NVIDIA’s Jensen Huang says that - 'IT will become the HR of AI agents'. Source

Epoch

Creating AI agents begins with defining their purpose and value. Managing their lifecycle—from development to deployment—can be complex and challenging. When it comes to SAP S/4HANA, finding a ready-made solution for AI agents is rare. However, thanks to NexGen.AI, we now have SAP AI Agents that can address some of these challenges. In this blog series, I’ll share how I built an AI agent group in 2024 that sped up SAP processes and significantly reduced manual effort.

 

The Challenge 

One of the biggest hurdles we encountered daily was dealing with unstructured emails. Automating this process wasn’t straightforward because:

  1. Emails had to be monitored, categorized, and forwarded to the right team.
  2. Team members manually extracted information from emails to process them further.
  3. This created a heavy workload, particularly for email categorization and processing file attachments.
  4. Additionally, each email needed to be acknowledged with an appropriate response.

To address these issues, we identified the need for advanced automation in pre-SAP processes. Unfortunately, the SAP ecosystem offers limited solutions that integrate SAP-specific knowledge with automation capabilities.

By leveraging NexGen AI Agents and SAP Build Process automations, we developed a solution to handle incoming emails intelligently. This AI agent could automatically sort emails, extract key information, process attachments, and send accurate responses—all with minimal human intervention.

This project demonstrated how AI agents can streamline and accelerate SAP processes, saving time and reducing effort while improving overall efficiency.

 

Is the AI-Agent already here from the Movie 'Matrix'?  

The moment has arrived, and we’ve entered a quiet tussle with AI. As humans, we just can’t resist filling up empty spaces—if we see one, we’re all over it. Remember that one person who hilariously claimed ownership of the Moon? Well, I spotted a free space in AI agent integrations with SAP business processes—and, of course, I couldn’t resist jumping in to make the most of it!

Working with AI agents is a bit like having a pet dog you adore. At first, the dog is clueless about life’s purpose but is very clear about its love for food—and you love it despite its chaos. But as any dog owner knows, with daily training and patience, even the most scatterbrained pup can learn new tricks.

AI agents are no different. Start them off with small, simple tasks so they can learn quickly and perform better. By breaking down big problems into bite-sized pieces, we can train these “AI puppies” to become specialized pros, equipped with the right tools to nail every task. Bonus: no chewed-up shoes!

Now, here’s how we’ve planned to show off these AI agents and their superpowers in automating business processes like pros!

The image above provides an overview of the high-level process flow and scope. The AI agents I have used or prepared are out-of-the-box (OOB) solutions from different platforms.

Application Scopes

The model integrates with:

  • Outlook
  • Azure
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • ServiceNow

Agentic Tools in Scope

The AI agent functionalities are built using:

  • Azure Logic App
  • SAP Build 
  • LangChain Framework
  • SAP AI Core - SAP Generative AI Hub

Overview of AI Agents

The process begins with an inbound email received at your business email address.

AI Agent: Email Analyzer

This agent leverages Cognitive AI and fine-tuned large language models to analyze emails and extract key details, such as:

  • Email Intent
  • Details of Email Attachments
  • OCR Processing for Attachments

AI Agent: Business Analyst

This agent is a collection of multiple mini-agents equipped with specialized tools to perform a variety of tasks. In this scenario, the tools include:

  • SAP OData AI: Extracts user-requested information and converts it into API calls.
  • SAP Workflow Tool: Creates workflows in SAP based on the inbound email.
  • ServiceNow Integration: Directly interacts with ServiceNow APIs to extract information and provide responses.
  • RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation): Connects to a knowledge base to handle user queries effectively.

AI Agent: Email Responder

This agent uses Generative AI to craft email responses. After processing inputs from previous agents, it generates a reply that is both accurate and context-aware. With the right prompts, it achieves results that surpass traditional solutions like ChatGPT, providing seamless and efficient communication.

This streamlined approach ensures end-to-end automation for business processes, making them faster and more efficient. 

Now, let’s dive into these two key components (and sprinkle in a bit of humor):

Shared Memory

This is where the magic happens. Shared memory is like the group chat for all the agents—except it’s actually productive. Think of it as the baton in a relay race: when one agent finishes its leg, it hands over the processed info to the next agent, saying, “Your turn, buddy!” With an organized shared memory template, the whole system runs smoother than a well-trained pet dog (you know, the one that finally stops chewing your shoes and starts fetching them).

Processing Logs

Now, logs might sound boring, but trust me, they’re the unsung heroes here. Imagine having a diary where every decision the LLM agent makes is recorded—complete with timestamps and all the juicy details. The best way to do this? Build a CAP application and store logs periodically. Not only does this give you confidence (“Hey, we’ve got receipts for that decision!”), but it also saves you when things go sideways. If the AI messes up, you’ve got a central “oops archive” to figure out what went wrong and fix it.

Revisiting the Steps:

1. New Email Arrival
A new email lands in your Outlook or Gmail inbox, triggering the event listener. This fires off a chain reaction to kickstart the process.

2. Email Analyzer Agent
The first stop in the chain is the Email Analyzer agent. It extracts all the relevant details from the email—like intent, attachments, or critical data—and passes this information to the next agent, the Business Analyst.

3. Business Analyst Agent
This is the MVP of the process. The Business Analyst agent takes the extracted information (a mix of structured and semi-structured input) and applies a “Chain of Thought” approach to generate outputs tailored to the input. Whether it’s interacting with your ERP systems like SAP S/4HANA or ECC, or connecting with ServiceNow and similar tools, this agent gets the job done.

4. Email Responder Agent
Finally, we reach the closing act. The Email Responder agent steps in to craft a polished response, ensuring the process ends with a well-written reply.

 

Is this the End of B2B APIs?

AI agents and event-driven systems aren’t replacing B2B APIs—they’re changing how we use them. APIs are essential for securely sharing data between systems, but their role is evolving. Instead of just being fixed tools for specific tasks, APIs now support smarter, AI-driven processes. Think of APIs as the foundation, while AI agents bring flexibility and intelligence. This shift doesn’t make APIs less important—it makes them more powerful and adaptable for today’s business needs.

What about this Business Analyst Agent?

Haha! AI agents can be a bit chaotic at times. As a real Business Analyst, it’s your job to keep an eye on them. These agents often struggle to figure out the next steps or process certain data correctly. Your role is to guide and channel their efforts to make them work effectively. The good news? These AI agents are here to handle the blunt, repetitive work, freeing you up to focus on solving critical business problems. While their responses are around 50–60% accurate, they’re a helpful starting point with your expertise leading the way!

 

feel free to share your thought..

entry.title: AI in SAP Build - Overview and Best Practices
entry.content:
AI in SAP Build: Transforming Business Applications

Introduction

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing the way businesses operate, and SAP Build is at the forefront of this transformation. SAP Build integrates AI capabilities to enhance application development, process automation, and overall business efficiency. This blog post provides an overview of AI in SAP Build and shares best practices in prompt engineering to help you maximize the potential of AI tools.

AI in SAP Build

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is revolutionizing the way businesses operate, and SAP Build is at the forefront of this transformation. SAP Build integrates AI capabilities to enhance application development, process automation, and overall business efficiency. This blog post provides an overview of AI in SAP Build and shares best practices in prompt engineering to help you maximize the potential of AI tools. 

What is SAP Build? 

SAP Build provides you the flexibility to create and extend apps, automate processes and design digital workspaces with low-code, pro-code and generative AI tools. Its collaborative design allows diverse teams to build solutions faster using prebuilt solutions, AI assistance, and seamless integration with SAP applications. 

General Topic: Best Practices in Prompt Engineering 

Before we start with all the AI features in detail, we have to cover a general topic. Prompt engineering is the art of crafting effective prompts to guide AI models in generating accurate and relevant outputs. For example prompts should be clear, specific, and directive. In this blog you can find all the details about the topic: Prompt Engineering

AI in SAP Build 

Coming to AI in SAP Build. SAP Build is a comprehensive suite designed to simplify the creation and customization of business applications. By incorporating AI, SAP Build empowers users to develop intelligent applications with minimal coding. This is a collection auf all the AI Capabilities in SAP Build. 

SAP Build Code 

  • Overview of all GenAI Options in SAP Build Code

 SAP Build Apps 

  • SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core  

SAP Build Work Zone

  • Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Process Automation (coming soon)

  • Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation  
  • GenAI boosting SAP Build Process Automation  

SAP Build - ABAP Environments (coming soon)

  • ABAP AI  

Joule Studio  (coming soon)

  • TechEd Session about Joule Studio 

 

Conclusion 

AI in SAP Build is transforming the way businesses develop and customize applications, making it easier to integrate intelligent features and automate processes. By following best practices in prompt engineering, you can harness the full potential of AI tools, ensuring they deliver high-quality and relevant outputs. Embrace these strategies to enhance your AI interactions and drive innovation in your business. 

This blog will be constantly updated. 

Share your experience using the AI capabilities in SAP Build in the comments!

entry.title: New "Wait for API Call" Integrates Processes with External Systems
entry.content:
SAP Build Process Automation has introduced the "Wait for an API Call" step, that pauses a process instance and waits for some system – any system – to call a specific API to resume the process instance. 

NOTE: The step was scheduled to be introduced this week and next to all the SAP BTP landscapes, but there are no guarantees, and the release could be delayed.

 

Why Use It

I believe the idea behind this is a scenario where, from your process, you call an external system that must execute some task before the rest of the process resumes.

For example:

  • A process receives an event from S/4HANA of a new employee/BP.
  • Some approval steps and other steps are executed.
  • Once approved, the process calls a Badge system to create an entry badge for the new person to get into the premises.
  • That Badge system does what it does.
  • When the Badge system is finished, it calls the API connected to the "Wait for an API Call" step, which resumes the process. 

P.S.: This is the idea behind our new SAP Build CodeJam.

 

How It Works

Add "Wait for an API Call" Step

Simply click the + sign to add a step, and go to Controls and Events area.

Then select the Wait for an API Call step.

You then give the trigger a name.

That's all you need to do.

If you want the API call that resumes the process to include additional data, you can define "input" parameters. The UI is the same as when specifying Process Inputs.

 

Create API Key

When calling the API for resuming the process instance, you must provide an API key with the proper permissions.

Go to the Control Tower, enter your environment, and select API Keys. 

Click Add API Key.

  1. Give the key a name.
  2. Give the key the following scopes:
    • trigger_read
    • trigger_execute

When calling the API, you must send the API key under a header called irpa-api-key.

 

Call the API 

Once you deploy the process to an environment, a separate trigger is created in that environment for that step -- in the same way an API trigger is created to trigger a new process instance.

Go into your environment, then to the Triggers tab. You'll see a trigger for your wait step.

Select the trigger and click View.

This will show you API details that are slightly different than the API details for the standard process API trigger.

The URL:

  • Standard API Trigger: A fixed URL (for each landscape). For EU10, for example, it is https://spa-api-gateway-bpi-eu-prod.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instances
  • Wait Trigger: The URL path is different and it includes a unique GUID that defines that specific trigger. If you create more than one Wait trigger in a process, they will have different GUIDs. I am not aware of a way to get this GUID at runtime.

The payloads are also different.

  • Standard API Trigger: Contains a definitionId (plus all the process inputs) so the system knows which process you want to trigger.
  • Wait Trigger: Contains an executionId (plus any inputs defined for the Wait trigger) so the system knows which process instance needs to be resumed.

That API can only be used if the specified process instance is actually waiting for that specific API call.

Remember to send your API key under the irpa-api-key header.

 

Getting the Process Instance ID 

You will need to send the process instance ID to the 3rd-party system that will resume the process instance, so they can include it in the request body of the API.

One way to do this is to create a custom variable to hold the instance ID.

Add Script Task to get the instance ID from the info object and put it in the custom variable.

 

Deleting Wait Triggers

A kind of quirky behavior occurs when you delete a "Wait" trigger and then redeploy – AND you have process instances still running from the previous version of your process.

👉If you delete the "Wait" trigger, what happens to those process instances?

Well, no worries. 

The old processes will continue to run and the old "Wait" trigger can still be used. It will continue to appear in the Control Tower.

When the last process that uses that trigger is completed, that "Wait" trigger will disappear from the Control Tower.

 

Errors

I have not re-created all the potential errors, but so far have figured out when the following status codes are returned when calling the "Wait" API.

  • 204: Success
  • 400: The GUID in the URL does not represent an existing "Wait" trigger, or you did not send an API key.
  • 403: The process instance ID passed in the request body does not exist or that process instance is not waiting for an API call.

This is just based on a few minutes testing, so it may not be 100% accurate. And there may be cases when the API returns other status codes. If you experience any, please let us all know.

entry.title: Part 1: SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR): Foundation for Modern Revenue Management
entry.content:
In today's complex business environment, managing revenue recognition has become increasingly challenging, especially with the introduction of standards like IFRS 15 and ASC 606. Organizations across industries are grappling with intricate revenue streams, diverse contract types, and stringent compliance requirements. SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) emerges as a comprehensive solution to these challenges, providing automated and standardized revenue recognition processes that ensure compliance while improving efficiency.

The Evolution of Revenue Recognition

Revenue recognition has transformed significantly from simple invoice-based accounting to a sophisticated process that considers multiple performance obligations, complex pricing structures, and varying recognition patterns. SAP RAR addresses these modern requirements by providing a robust foundation built on three core pillars: contract management, revenue recognition engine, and seamless integration capabilities.

 

Understanding the Core Process

The journey of revenue recognition in SAP RAR represents a sophisticated orchestration of multiple components working together to ensure accurate and compliant revenue processing. Let's explore this through a comprehensive real-world scenario that demonstrates the system's capabilities.

Consider TechServe Solutions, a technology company that offers both hardware and subscription-based software services. They've recently closed a deal that includes:

  • Enterprise Server Hardware: $100,000
  • Software License: $50,000/year for 3 years
  • Implementation Services: $30,000
  • Maintenance Support: $20,000/year for 3 years

Contract Inception and RAI Creation

When the sales order is created, SAP RAR begins its sophisticated processing journey. The system automatically generates Revenue Accounting Items (RAIs) for each component of the deal. These RAIs serve as the foundation for all subsequent revenue recognition processes. During this phase, the system captures critical information including contract terms, pricing details, and delivery schedules.

Performance Obligation Breakdown

SAP RAR's intelligent contract analysis capabilities automatically identify and separate distinct performance obligations within the contract. 

Price Allocation and SSP Determination

One of the most complex aspects of revenue recognition is the fair allocation of transaction prices. SAP RAR handles this through its sophisticated Standalone Selling Price (SSP) engine. For TechServe's contract:

The total contract value of $280,000 ($100,000 + $150,000 + $30,000 + $60,000) is allocated based on relative SSP values. SAP RAR automatically calculates these allocations while considering:

  • Market-based pricing data
  • Historical transaction analysis
  • Contract-specific adjustments
  • Volume discounts and bundling impacts

Revenue Recognition Scheduling

Based on the performance obligations and price allocations, SAP RAR creates a detailed revenue recognition schedule. This automated process considers:

  1. Time-Based Recognition The software license and maintenance support revenues are scheduled over 36 months, with automatic recognition triggers at each period end.
  2. Event-Based Recognition Hardware revenue recognition is linked to delivery completion, while implementation revenue follows project milestone achievements.
  3. Multi-Element Considerations The system manages interdependencies between different performance obligations, ensuring proper revenue timing and allocation.

Financial Integration and Posting

The final stage involves the seamless integration with financial systems through a three-step process:

This robust process ensures that every revenue dollar is properly recognized, tracked, and reported according to the relevant accounting standards while maintaining complete audit trails and documentation.

Technical Architecture and Integration

At the heart of SAP RAR lies a sophisticated technical architecture that ensures seamless processing of revenue recognition requirements. The system integrates with various source systems through the Adapter Reuse Layer (ARL), which standardizes incoming data into Revenue Accounting Items (RAIs). These items then flow through the BRFplus rules engine, where business logic determines their treatment.

 

The Role of Artificial Intelligence

The integration of artificial intelligence in SAP RAR represents a significant leap forward in revenue recognition capabilities. Modern implementations leverage machine learning algorithms to analyze contract terms, predict revenue patterns, and identify potential recognition issues before they occur. Natural language processing capabilities help in automatically extracting key terms from contracts, while predictive analytics provide insights into future revenue trends.

Implementation Impact

Organizations implementing SAP RAR experience transformative benefits across their revenue operations. Manual processing time typically reduces by up to 80%, while recognition accuracy improves to near-perfect levels. The system's ability to handle complex revenue scenarios while maintaining compliance with IFRS 15 and ASC 606 provides organizations with confidence in their financial reporting.

Future Outlook

The future of revenue recognition in SAP RAR is closely tied to the advancement of intelligent technologies. As artificial intelligence and machine learning capabilities continue to evolve, we can expect even more sophisticated features in contract analysis, revenue prediction, and compliance automation. The system's ability to learn from historical patterns and adapt to new business scenarios will become increasingly valuable for organizations managing complex revenue streams.

Conclusion

SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting represents more than just a technical solution; it embodies a modern approach to revenue management that combines automation, intelligence, and compliance. As businesses continue to evolve with new revenue models and complex contracts, SAP RAR's robust foundation and forward-looking capabilities ensure that organizations can confidently manage their revenue recognition processes both today and in the future.

The next chapter discusses the Business AI component built on SAP BTP services to make RAR fully autonomous. Don’t forget to check it out! 😎
Part 2: Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) wit... - SAP Community

#SAP #RevenueRecognition #SAPRAR #IFRS15 #ASC606 #FinanceAutomation #RevenueManagement #ArtificialIntelligence #MachineLearning #Compliance #AccountingStandards #TechInnovation #BusinessEfficiency #DigitalTransformation #FinancialReporting #Automation #DataDriven #FutureOfFinance #ContractManagement #PerformanceObligations #AIinFinance #SAPSolutions #FinancialCompliance #RevenueAutomation #TechServeSolutions

entry.title: Configure Journal Entry Posting Approval from Store Template in Build Process Automation
entry.content:
In our case we were unable to utilize the Secure Network Connection (SNC) option, instead, we opted for a basic cloud connector connection to connect S/4 HANA on-premise system, which presented its own set of challenges. By referring to multiple external resources and blogs, we were able to successfully navigate through the configuration process and resolve the issues we encountered.

We hope that this blog post will serve as a valuable resource for anyone facing similar challenges in configuring journal entry posting from BPA Store. Let's get started

 

Step1

Prerequisites

The following SAP Business Technology Platform services are required to consume the content package Journal Entry Posting Approval:

 

  • SAP Build Process Automation to orchestrate the process.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
  • SAP S/4HANA, private edition
  • SAP Business Technology Platform, Cloud Foundry runtime
  • SAP Work Zone, standard or advanced edition for accessing My Inbox and Process Visibility workspaces.
  • SAP Business Application Studio for SAP UI5 application 
  • SAP Build Process Automation instance
  • SAP Document Management Service
  • SAP Desktop Agent 3.19 or higher – need to install in desktop or laptop.

 

Step 2

Destination Configuration

  1. Setup S/4 HANA - S4HANA_JOURNALENTRY_MG4

Pointing to S4 HANA on-premises system. And it should be configured in cloud connector. You have to use virtual host and port configured in cloud connector in URL.

 

Name

S4HANA_JOURNALENTRY_MG4

Type

HTTP

Proxy Type

On-Premise

User

<Onpremise-User>

Password

<Onpremise-Password>

Authentication

Basic Authentication

URL

<URL configured in Cloud connector for S4 HANA>

Additional Properties

 

HTML5.DynamicDestination: true,

sap-client:<S4 Client>,

sap-platform: abap,

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled: true,

sap.processautomation.enabled: true,

WebIDEEnabled: true,

WebIDEUsage: odata_abap,dev_abap

Once created destination, check connection. You should get below success message

This template uses APIs and communication scenarios & these APIs are needed to be configured in SAP S/4HANA. The detail of these APIs  and required roles , cloud connector configuration you can refer configuration guide given in store from Page No 7 to 15.

  1. Document Management

The Input Template document for Sales Contract and any supporting documents can be uploaded in the approval form which are then made available as attachments for review and approval to the respective participants. For this you will need document management service and a corresponding destination in SAP BTP to connect to repository . But this step is optional.   

Create Instance for Document Management Service, under Instances and subscriptions. 

Go to service instances and search for Document Management Service, Integration Option. Click instances of DMS. You will see instance details as below

Under Service Keys, click three dots which will show popup with View option. Click View.

Copy highlighted values from service key as per below screenshot

Please follow below help link for further information

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-repository-in-sap-document-management-service

Once subscribed to DMS, create destination named "sap_process_automation_document_store" and bind the respective values for client id, client secret & URL from document management service instance. 

Name

sap_process_automation_document_store

Type

HTTP

URL

Endpoints.ecmservice.url

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

Oauth2ClientCredentials

Client ID

uaa.clientid

Client Secret

uaa.clientsecret

Token Service URL

uaa.url + /oauth/token

3. SAP BPA Destination

sap_process_automation_service

If you are already having build process automation subscription, by default sap_process_automation_service destination will be available under destintation. Otherwise you can create by following below steps.

From BTP Cockpit select Instances and Subscription . Search for SAP Build Process Automation.

Select instance.

Click three dots in instance key -> Select View

Create destination by using service key details highlighted in screenshot. Respective path also given below.

 

Name

sap_process_automation_service

Type

HTTP

URL

Endpoint.api + /workflow/rest/v1

 

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

Oauth2ClientCredentials

ClientID

uaa.clientid

Client Secret

uaa.clientsecret

Token Service URL

 

Additional Properties

·         AppgyverEnabled: true

·         Endpoints: { "api":   <endpoints.api>}

·         html5-apps-repo:  {         "app_host_id": <html5-apps-repo.app_host_id>}

·         HTML5.DynamicDestination: true

·         Saasregistryenabled: true

·         sap.cloud.service: com.sap.spa.processautomation

·         sap.cloud.service.alias: spa

·         sap.processautomation.enabled: true

·         WebIDEEnabled: true

Remember while copying html5-apps-repo copy entire value starting from{.

Ex :  { “hostid” , “host id}

Similar way for endpoints copy entire value  {

Ex: {         "api":  <endpoint>     }

And then check connection. You will see below message.

4. SAP Build Process Automation Email

Configure email destination which will be used to send email to the approver, requestor, and admin at different stages of the process execution

Follow the steps to Configure a SMTP Mail Destination in your BTP account.

https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configuring-smtp-mail-destination

Step 3

 

Configure APIs in SAP S/4HANA

This template uses the following APIs and communication scenarios & these APIs are needed to be configured in SAP S/4HANA. The detail of these APIs and required roles , cloud connector configuration you can refer configuration guide given in store from Page No 7 to 15

Step 4

Create Project from template in BPA

 

Goto Build Process Automation Cockpit, in left side pane, you can see Store

Click on Store - > and Enter Journal Entry Posting in search bar. Select first project in which publisher is SAP Build Process Automation.

You will navigate to this below page, where description of project, artifacts involved, configuration guide, required dependencies detailed out

You can download configuration guide by clicking pdf icon under Documents section

Now choose Create from Template option in top right corner of the page. You will get below Popup. Give your project name and click create.

Now from lobby you can see project got created. Click on project name.

You will be taken to overview page

Click icon nearby Overview. And you get to see artifacts overview in the below format.

Step 5

Deploy Project

Once imported, release and deploy from the Lobby. Before deployment, have to do some changes in determine approver step to change approver email id of workflow.

Configuration guide has brief steps from page 16 to 18. You can follow the same steps.

In journal Entry posting, action group created based on SAP SNC (Single Sign On) and SAP basic connection.

Either one of them required to run application. If you are using cloud connector configuration SNC is not required in this case.

Document Check – Using SAP basic connection

Document Check (SNC) – Using Secure Network Communication

Document Post – Using SAP basic connection

Document Post(SNC) - Using Secure Network Communication

In workflow, SNC based action group is used. Have to change it to basic connection based action group.

Replace existing SNC action group in (Journal Entry Document Check) automation with Document Check

For that, from artifacts select Journal Entry Document Check highlighted as below

In the below screen, remove action group BAPI DOCUMENT_CHECK (SNC). Drag and drop BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_CHECK under Document Check as below.

Fill in required parameters in right pane.

Parameters:

Connection Type

Basic

Message Server

<Backend System server IP>

Instance Number

<Backend System Instance No>

Client

<Backend System Client>

User

< Backend System login user>

Password

< Backend System login password>

Next step is bind the value for BAPIResponse. Once replaced SNC with Document Check, will get error in some of steps. Let’s resolve.  Click step Set Variable: BAPI Response. Select edit icon highlighted below in screenshot

Edit expression as per below screenshot

Similar way change set Variable document Error by following screenshots.

Go to Overview page of Journal Entry Posting and select Journal Entry Document Post automation as highlighted below.

Delete current action group in step 5 and drag and drop BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST in same step.

Select step 5. And fill details like message server (s4 hana system IP address), instance number, client, username and password

Once changed document post, will get some errors in other steps. Follow below steps to fix errors. Click step no 8 and in right pane, select highlighted icon to edit value

Change value as per below screenshot

Select step 9. And edit value from right pane.

Change expression as per below screenshot and save.

Journal Document Entry Post Automation:

Post completing all the changes, process needs to be released and deployed from SAP Build to reflect the changes.

While deploying you will get below popup, choose destination which is pointing to cloud connector URL of S4 HANA System.

Step 6

Deploy UI5 applications into BTP Cockpit

From artifacts, we have to deploy ui5 application into BTP cockpit from Business Application Studio.

Click Journal-Entry-Posting-JournalEntryApproval_ui.tar. Click Download button in below screen. Similarly download Journal-Entry-Posting-JournalEntryApproval_ui.tar as well.

Go to Business Application Studio. Extract downloaded mtar file. From BAS Click File-> Import Project -> select extracted project zip file.

Open mta.yaml file and change SBPA-instance to your build process automation instance name . You can find instance name under instances in BTP cockpit  as below.

 

SAP Build Process Automation as the successor product of SAP Workflow Management has Workflow, Business Rules and Process Visibility capabilities of Workflow Management

In this, we are going to use Build process automation capabilities not workflow management.So we have to replace bpmworkflowruntime destination with BPA destination.

Go to your xs-app.json.  change 1st route pointing to bpmworkflowruntime with below

{

       "source": "^/sap_process_automation_service/(.*)$",

        "target": "$1",

        "destination": "sap_process_automation_service",

        "authenticationType": "xsuaa",

        "csrfProtection": false

    },

Once  replaced,  your xs-app.json will look like below .

Destination S4HANA_JOURNALENTRY_MG4 which we have created earlier in cockpit for S4 HANA on-premise system.

 sap_process_automation_service is destination which will be created by default while you are running booster for build process automation.Go to CreateView.Controller.

Existing

In getWorkflowRuntimeBaseURL change url as below

URL in destination has path till v1<bpa endpoint>/workflow/rest/v1

So just replace /bpmworkflowruntime/public/workflow/rest/v1

Go to startWorkflowInstance function and replace definitionId with yours. You can get definition id from below path SAP BPA->Monitoring - >Processes and Workflows .

This definitionId will be available once you have deployed project .

Comment out header section. Token fetch call is configured in destination level. So this section not required in our case.

Once completed all the above changes deploy application into Cockpit.

 

Another mtar file named Journal-Entry-Posting-JournalEntryApproval_ui.tar. Extract it and import zip file into BAS. Then deploy. No changes required in approval level.  In case you need additional information add it and deploy.

 

 

 

Step 7

Agent Registration

Before running application, go to desktop agent installed in your local machine. Select Settings -> mode and activate Unattended mode. Because Document Check and Document Post are unattended automation which will be triggered in background once we submitted journal entry from UI5 application.

Next step have to add our agent to agent management. Go to build process automation lobby. Choose Control Tower then Agents under Agent Configuration.

Select Register Agent

After registration, you can see your laptop hostname under Items.

For further details on this , please follow this link https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_ONE/68a2e87fb29941b5bf959a184d9c6727/972ce532e66d48d8a7b43236fbb50248.html

Step 8

Testing Journal Entry Posting

Good to go. Now we can run application directly from deployed url in BTP -> HTML5 applications.

If you want to configure Journal Entry ui5 application in Build work zone standard edition, please follow the steps in this blog

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/integrate-with-work-zone..html

To enable My Inbox, Process Workspace, Monitor Workflows, follow the steps in this blog

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-configure-workzone.html

 

Go to BTP Cockpit. Choose HTML5 applications. Search for Journal

Click JournalEntryStartUimodule. Once application loaded, you could see below screen.

You have two options to post journal entry. Either manually enter data in form or upload excel. Excel template you can find in Project artifacts

Choose Upload in Posting Via drop down. Then upload file and submit.

Once submitted, from build process automation lobby, go to monitoring and select highlighted icon as shown in pic

My Inbox application will open in another tab.

In My Inbox, you can see new task. Enter required comments  and Approve the task.

Incase any issues, you didn’t get any task in My Inbox, from lobby go to monitoring -> Click Process and Workflow Instances.

In instances filter definition choose journal entry posting approval process , instance filter status select all . Tasks will be listed down. Choose task and see flow of execution and where it got failed

Step 9

Check journal entries from Fiori Launchpad

Open SAP Fiori launchpad from backend system. From launchpad select highlighted tile. And you can see journal entry posted from our end. Successfully we have configured Journal Entry .

Successfully configured journal entry posting from bot store.

entry.title: How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Yesterday, while presenting SAP’s financial results for FY2024, our CEO Christian Klein explained that we exceeded our cloud goals, accelerating cloud revenue and current cloud backlog growth against a much larger base. He also mentioned that half of our cloud order entry are now including AI. Clearly, our aim is to provide our customers with a suite of cloud-based applications that provides the most valuable data in business and powers unique AI capabilities. In this blog post, I would like to present how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite – both running on SAP Business Technology Platform – are at the heart of SAP’s strategy and support our customers in their journey from legacy ERPs to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.


We know that for many SAP customers, the transition to SAP S/4HANA can be challenging, because someone in the organization has added a lot of custom code to your ERP 😉. That’s why SAP has created RISE with SAP, a packaged offering that helps you transform on-premise SAP ERP systems, modernize business processes, and become more agile in the cloud. And in RISE with SAP, you will find anything you need to fulfill your needs.

Let me be a bit more specific: every company in the world uses software to run their operations. We all know the most efficient and effective way is to use standard software like our SAP S/4HANA ERP. At the same time, we are fully aware that every organization in the world is unique and gains competitive advantage through their very specific processes. This might be the way they run processes for their customers because they offer unique experiences, or the way they engage with their suppliers because they may be faster in the supply chain processes, or it might be their HR processes which allows them to retain the best talent in the market… whatever it is, that makes your company unique, it should be reflected in your software because no one likes manual processes in 2025. In the context of migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, following the Clean Core strategy is a powerful opportunity: by eliminating custom code, technical debt, and standardizing the processes and data that underpin ERP operations, your business will benefit from more best practices while being able to customize what it needs to differentiate itself. And when it comes to standardization to achieve clean core with proper extensions and integrations, SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP enter the game!

SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite add great value to your Cloud ERP

SAP Build allows you to extend and personalize SAP S/4HANA to meet your needs. It accelerates development and automation of extensions with low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. It enables stable, secure, and scalable ERP extensions that adhere to a prescribed extensibility model, with compliance to code quality standards and best practices. Tailored for every SAP customer, any size, any landscape, any industry, SAP Build is the best of suite tools for developers and key users collaborating in AI-powered ERP extension development.

Let me say it simply: SAP Build adds great value to your Cloud ERP. You will get more out of your cloud ERP investment with SAP Build. Just because it lets you easily customize, extend, integrate and automate business processes in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution to meet specific requirements while keeping the core clean. Watch our webinar “Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build: your Free Toolkit”: in an hour, you will discover how you can leverage SAP Build’s low-code, pro-code, and generative AI development tools to drive innovation, automate processes, and supercharge your S/4HANA Cloud experience.

SAP Integration Suite is an iPaaS that helps you quickly integrate on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. For sure, it also adds value to your ERP: it connects business processes with proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices. It provides upgrade-stable interfaces, proper monitoring and error resolution capabilities, and actively used and well-documented connectors.

For What Use Cases?

Let me give you very concrete examples where SAP Build is very helpful to address specific challenges in your Lead to Cash processes:

  • Digitize in-store cash sales processing
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from Excel files
  • Automate mass maintenance of billing blocks
  • Automate the approvals of return requests
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from unstructured data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Automate managing credit blocks on sales orders

and more…

I can also share many use cases that could improve your Source to Pay processes:

  • Centralize and streamline supplier communications with dedicated business sites
  • Mass maintenance of purchase contracts
  • Automate the creation of purchase requisitions from structured data in SAP S/4HANA
  • Automate the processing of paper-based invoices
  • Automatically populate missing material codes in purchase requisitions
  • Track sustainability data across your supply chain

and more…

You will find complementary use cases on our SAP Build web page.

And there is more: on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you can browse 500+ pre-built content packages for SAP Build and 600+ pre-built content packages for SAP Integration Suite. If you have developed custom code in your on-premises ERP in the past to address such use cases or specific needs, you can explore this vast catalog to see if some of these packages might be a good fit for your cloud ERP migration process. Indeed it is very important to know that SAP Build comes with pre-built content for ECC: you can use these SAP Build packages on ECC already to standardize some extensions, and then all the extensions developed on ECC with SAP Build will taken to S/4HANA during the migration.

Insights from Customers

What I presented above is not only theoretical: customers who have been through this SAP S/4HANA journey share valuable insights that can really help you. I have selected two few interesting stories, in different industries.

Sony Pictures

Let me start with Sony Pictures Networks: relying on the legacy system, SAP ECC, their highly customized and siloed infrastructure struggled to align with the company's integrated business processes. And decentralized data presented SAP challenges in consolidating business reports until the end.

Sony decided to remaster their operations for the future through next-generation ERP, with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and Enterprise Automation, combining SAP Signavio, SAP Build And SAP Integration Suite. Indeed, SAP Signavio radically accelerates business process transformation, and I engage you to read this blog post explaining how customers on SAP ECC systems can prepare for an SAP S/4HANA transformation with SAP Signavio.

“By migrating to SAP S/4HANA and adopting SAP Business Technology Platform, we can keep the core clean. This strategy allows for faster development of any new technologies or any new process rollouts and enables us to take advantage of generative AI as part of our way forward” explains Mahesh Patil, SVP and Head of IT – Infrastructure, Integration, and Data Lake, Sony Pictures Networks India. And the results are excellent:

  • 100% automated process mining, reducing effort from weeks to days
  • 50% of interfaces managed through SAP Integration Suite
  • ~85% faster mobile app creation, reducing effort from four weeks to three days

Hitachi

Would you like another good example? Let’s take Hitachi High-Tech Corporation: they replaced their highly customized multinational business landscape by investing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in a two-tier public and private cloud model. With a fit-to-standard ethos, the company can now focus on supporting business innovation across its subsidiaries using a side-by-side approach on SAP Business Technology Platform.

“Our new policy is to fit our business processes to standard as much as possible, reducing our need for customizations” says Yuri Yasuda, Manager Corporate DX Department, Digital Transformation Business Group, Hitachi High-Tech Corporation, who shares impressive KPIs:

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x faster handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

You can also find inspiration in the S/4HANA projects that Mahindra Group, PX Energy, LEOCH, Farys, Delta Light or Prestige have successfully implemented.

Start your S/4HANA journey with the support of SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite!

I hope that you’re now convinced that SAP BTP in general and SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in particular are very powerful to ease your migration to SAP S/4HANA. To start this journey, watch our short video and request a customized demo: together, let’s make your business unstoppable!

entry.title: SAP Build AppsでSAP Document Management Serviceへファイルをアップロードし、SAP Build Process Automationでそれを使う手法 その２
entry.content:
※この記事はその１からの続き物です。

2. SAP Build Apps側の作成

まず、Application Variableとして変数を2つ設定します。

FolderIdとFolderNameです。この2つをテキスト型で作成します。Application VariableでなくPage Variableでも問題ないので実際の用途に合わせて使い分けてください。

SAP Build Apps側はこんな感じの単純な画面を用意しました。

ボタン2つだけです。ボタンのラベルの内容から何を行うかは推測できると思いますが、それぞれのロジックを作っていきます。

その前に、その1で解説したSAP Build Appsの新しい機能をインストールします。
MARKET PLACEに進み、「HTTP destination」と検索し

HTTP destination requestというモジュールをインストールしてください。同様の手順で「Pick Files」とも検索し、「Pick Files」コンポーネントもインストールします。

3.Upload Fileボタンのロジックの作成

それではUpload Fileボタンのロジックの作成です。
その１で説明した通り、SAP Build Process AutomationでDMSにアップロードしたファイルを使用するにはフォルダを作成し、そこにファイルをアップロードすることが必要です。ということでこのボタンでは

  1. アップロードするファイルの選択
  2. DMSにフォルダを作成（＆フォルダIDの取得）
  3. 2で作成したフォルダにファイルをアップロード

というロジックを書くことになります。ロジックの全体は以下のとおりです。

全て説明すると長くなるので、重要と思われる数字を振った部分のみ解説します。

１．Pick Filesコントロールでファイルを選ばせる。
　　ここでは１つのファイルしか選択できないようにOPTIONAL INPUTSのPick multiple files?をFalseに変更してください。

２．set app variableでDMS上のフォルダ名を作成し、変数：FolderNameに設定する。

Assigned valueですが、Formulaを使用し、今回は

FromBuildApps-という前置詞をつけたタイムスタンプ値をフォルダ名にします。おそらくはこれはは一意になると思います。

３．HTTP destination requestを使用して、DMS上にフォルダーを作成する。
HTTP destination requestを使用してDMS上にフォルダーを作成します。

Destinationはその１で作成した「sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps」を指定し、HTTP MethodはPOST、Request body typeとしてmultipart/form-dataを指定します。
Request bodyは"List of values"を使用して下記の表のように指定します。注意書きがない場合はStatic Textとして設定します。

keyvalue
cmisactioncreateFolder
propertyId[0]cmis:name
propertyValue[0]変数FolderNameを選択
propertyId[1]cmis:objectTypeId
propertyValue[1]cmis:folder
succincttrue

４．フォルダ作成APIの返り値からFolderId値を取得する。
３でDMS上にフォルダを作成した場合（成功した場合）、APIから以下のような値が返されます。

 

 

 

{
  "succinctProperties":
    {
      "cmis:objectId": "fWtCFDH4ah2BGeFqzoorOLETNNq65cmp82KfYUSpWxA",
      "sap:parentIds": ["5daf1b700094ead661934376"],
      "cmis:name": "FromBuildApps-1737086879955",
      "cmis:lastModifiedBy": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:objectTypeId": "cmis:folder",
      "cmis:lastModificationDate": 1737086882353,
      "cmis:createdBy": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:baseTypeId": "cmis:folder",
      "cmis:parentId": "5daf1b700094ead661934376",
      "sap:owner": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:creationDate": 1737086882353,
      "cmis:changeToken": "0",
    },
  "exactACL": false,
}

 

 

SAP Build Process Automationのプロセスへ送らなければならないフォルダのObjectIdは"cmis:objectId"の値です。この値を変数FolderIdに代入します。
これはFormulaを用い、
STRING(<フォルダを作成するHTTP destination requestの出力>.resBodyParsed.succinctProperties["cmis:objectId"])
と指定することで取得することが出来ます。<フォルダを作成するHTTP destination requestの出力>.resBodyParsedは左側に表示されるVariablesのoutput of another nodeから選択して入力すると間違いがないです。

５．DMSへファイルをアップロードする
これは３と同様にHTTP destination requestを使用してDMSのファイルアップロードAPIを操作します。
Destinationはその１で作成した「sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps」を指定し、HTTP MethodはPOST、Request body typeとしてmultipart/form-dataを指定します。


Request bodyは"List of values"を使用して下記の表のように指定します。注意書きがない場合はStatic Textとして設定します。

keyvalue
cmisactioncreateDocument
propertyId[0]cmis:name
propertyValue[0]

Formulaを使用して1のPickFilesのfiles[0].nameを指定

propertyId[1]cmis:objectTypeId
propertyValue[1]cmis:document
filenamePropertyValue[0]と同様にFormulaを使用して1のPickFilesのfiles[0].nameを指定
_charset_UTF-8
includeAllowableActionstrue
succinctfalse
mediaFormulaを使用して1のPickFilesのfiles[0]を指定

重要なポイントとしてDMSではファイルをフォルダにアップロードする場合、パスを指定する必要があります。これはAPIのエンドポイントにそのパス名（フォルダ名）が含まれる形です。従ってHTTP destination requestのOPTIONAL INPUTSを開き、PathにFormulaを使用して"/"+appVars.FolderNameという形でフォルダ名を追加したものが使われるようにします。

これでアップロードが出来ます。

File Uploadボタンのロジックはこれで完成です。次はこのアップロードしたファイルの情報を持ってSAP Build Process Automationのプロセスへ連携するExecute Workflowボタンのロジックの開発です。

長くなってしまいましたのでその３に続きます。

entry.title: SAP Build AppsでSAP Document Management Serviceへファイルをアップロードし、SAP Build Process Automationでそれを使う手法 その１
entry.content:
大分間が空いてしまいましたが、この記事は一応
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-apps%E3%81%A8sap-build-process-automation%E3%81%AE%E9%80%A3%E6%90%BA-%E3%83%95%E3%82%A9%E3%83%BC%E3%83%A0%E3%83%88%E3%83%AA%E3%82%AC%E3%83%BC%E3%81%AE%E5%A0%B4%E5%90%88/ba-p/13683267
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-apps-%E3%82%88%E3%82%8A%E7%B0%A1%E5%8D%98%E3%81%AB%E3%81%AA%E3%81%A3%E3%81%9Fsap-build-process-automation%E3%81%A8%E3%81%AE%E9%80%A3%E6%90%BA/ba-p/13682980
これらの記事の続き物です。

私は前者の方の記事で「ファイル添付機能を持ったSAP Build Process Automationトリガーフォームの代用としてSAP Build Appsを使用することは非常に厄介」という旨を説明しました。これはSAP Build AppsからSAP Document Management Serviceへのファイルのアップロードが非常に面倒（だった）というのが理由です。どのように解説しようかなと考えた上前説的な記事として
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/try-sending-data-in-multipart-form-data-format-on-sap-build-apps/ba-p/13949529
こちらを公開しましたが、私がかつて取っていた手法からすると遥かに簡単な方法でDMSへアップロードすることが出来る機能がSAP Build AppsのMARKET PLACEにて公開されましたのでそれを利用する方法で説明したいと思います。

前解説：SAP Build Process Automationのファイル添付機能について

SAP Build Process Automationでファイルの添付機能を利用した場合、ファイルはSAP Document Management Serviceに保存されます。SAP Document Management Service側でファイルがどのように格納されているか見たことはあるでしょうか？実はファイルごとにフォルダが作成され、そのフォルダーにファイルが格納されます。SAP Document Management Serviceでは管理上フォルダやファイル毎に一意のIDが付与されます。SAP Build Process Automation内では添付したファイルはファイルのIDではなく（自動作成された）フォルダのIDで扱います。これは正しくは
spa-res:cmis:folderid:<DMS上のフォルダID>
という形式で扱われます。

つまりは、SAP Build AppsでSAP Document Management Serviceにファイルをアップロードし、それをSAP Build Process Automationのワークフロー上で使用したいという場合、Build Apps側で
1. SAP Document Management Serviceのレポジトリ上にフォルダを作成する。その際にフォルダに対して発行されたIDを取得しておく。
2. 上記のフォルダにファイルをアップロードする
3. SAP Build AppsからSApBuild Process Automationのワークフローを起動する際、「spa-res:cmis:folderid:<DMS上のフォルダID>」という形式でファイルの保存先情報を渡す
ということが必要です。

前準備：Destinationを作成する

SAP Build AppsからSAP Document Management ServiceにアクセスするためにDestinationを作成します。これはSAP Build Process AutomationとSAP Document Management Serviceの連携のためにDestinationである「sap_process_automation_document_store」というDestinationをクローンして作成することにします。「sap_process_automation_document_store」にSAP Build Appsで使用するための追加プロパティを設定しても良いかなと考えたのですが、これは連携のために作り方が指定されている・自動的に作成されたDestinationであるため、ほぼ同一の内容となりますが、SAP Build Appsからの利用用にもう一つ作成することにします。

私は「sap_process_automation_document_store」をクローンして「sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps」というDestinationを作成しました。
このDestinationは「sap_process_automation_document_store」から以下の部分を変更しています。
URL:　一番後ろに /rootを追加
更にAdditional Propertiesとして以下を追加しています。

BuildApps.ApiTyperest
BuildApps.Enabledtrue
HTML5.DynamicDestinationtrue
MobileEnabledtrue

URLに/rootを追加したことに関しては正直必須ではないかなとも思います。しかしSAP Business Accelerator HubでDocument Management Serviceで使用するAPIを確認した際に/rootまで付与したURLが共通で使用できると判断したため労力を下げるために追加しました。Additional Propertiesに設定した項目はこのDestinationをSAP Build Appsで使用するための設定項目となります。

 

それでは簡単なアプリを作りながら解説します。

 

1. SAP Build Process Automation側のプロセスの作成

まず、添付ファイルの情報を受け取ることになるワークフローを作成します。単純なものを作成します。

トリガーとしてはAPIトリガーを使用し、以前解説した方法でSAP Build Appsから連携します。プロセスの入力変数としてFolderIdという

DocumentFolder型の変数を設定します。
ここからつながっているフォームも単純で

ファイルオブジェクトを配置します。このファイルオブジェクトはReadOnly属性にチェックを入れてください。
プロセス上でこのフォームに入力変数として設定したFolderIdを割り当てます。

フォームの件名や宛先などは適当に設定します。テストでは自分自身に来るように宛先を設定することにしましょう。宛先はProcess metadataの"Process started by"を指定します。
リリースしてデプロイします。SAP Build Appsから利用できるようにロビーから

Published to Libraryを選択してライブラリにパブリッシュすることを忘れずに。

 

長くなるのでその２に続きます。

entry.title: SAP Build AppsでSAP Document Management Serviceへファイルをアップロードし、SAP Build Process Automationでそれを使う手法 その３
entry.content:
※この記事はその２からの続き物です。その１はこちら。

その２まででDMSへフォルダを作成し、ファイルのアップロードを行うロジックが完成しましたので、今度はこのSAP Build AppsのアプリケーションからSAP Build Process Automationのプロセスを起動するロジックを作成します。アップロードしたファイル（フォルダ）の情報を付けて起動しますのでSAP Build Process Automation上でこのファイルの参照が出来るようになります。
基本的な方法は以前解説した方法と同じです。

SAP Build AppsではIntegrationsからSAP Build Libraryでその１で作成したプロセスを使用できるようにします。

ここで一覧にデプロイしたプロセスが出てこないという方、大体はPublishを忘れていますよ。

Execute Workflowボタンのロジックを作成します。ロジックとしては単純です。

重要なのはTrigger Processです。Integrationsから使えるようにしたプロセスを選択します。

Input ParametersとしてObject with Propertiesを選択し、Formulaを使用して値を設定します。

その１で解説したとおり、フォルダIDの情報は"spa-res:cmis:folderid:<DMS上のフォルダID>"という形式で渡す必要があります。フォルダIDはフォルダ作成時にAPIの返り値からApplication Variablesに代入したはずです。その値に"spa-res:cmis:folderid:"を文字列連結します。

これでロジックは完成です。テストしてみましょう。

 

テスト

作成したBuild Appsアプリケーションを実行します。

File Uploadボタンをクリックするとファイル選択のダイアログが開きますので、今回はPDF文書をアップロードします。テスト用に単純なPDF文書をWordで作成しました。

ファイルアップロードAPIの後に設定したダイアログの設定内容に依存しますが、とりあえずアップロード出来たようです。デバッグの状態ではちゃんとフォルダを作成しアップロード出来ているかReuseUIやDMSのAPIから確認したほうが良いでしょう。
では次にExecute Workflowボタンをクリックし、SAP Build Process Automationのプロセスを起動します。
このプロセス（その１で作成）はAPIでトリガーされた後、自分自身にファイルが添付されたフォームを送信する構造です。SAP Build LobbyからMy Inboxを開きます。

自分自身にフォームが届いています。添付ファイルもあるようです。中身を確認してみます。

SAP Build AppsからアップロードしたPDFファイルであることが確認できました。

このようにしてSAP Build Appsでファイルをアップロードし、SAP Build Process Automationのワークフローへ連携が可能です。以前解説した通り、SAP Build AppsではSAP Build Process Automationよりよりアプリケーション的な処理を行うことが出来ます。SAP Build Process Automationのフォームでは無理なこと、例えばも大分類→中分類→小分類→製品といった絞り込み検索や外部システムとのインタラクティブな連携も可能です。ワークフローのトリガーとなる申請画面にSAP Build Appsを使用するのはお勧めの使い方です。

また、"spa-res:cmis:folderid:"+フォルダIDという形式でプロセスをトリガする際に渡せば他のアプリからでもDMSにアップロードしたファイルを連携出来ると言うことも重要なポイントです。（SAP Build Process Automationで使用しているDMSレポジトリと同じレポジトリである必要はありますが）

お客様の使い方をお聞きする限り、ワークフローにファイルを添付するというのは多いパターンのようです。出来ることが大幅に増えると思いますのでぜひやってみてください。

entry.title: How to upload files to DMS using SAP Build Apps and use them in SAP Build Process Automation:Part1
entry.content:
I often get asked, "Can I use SAP Build Apps to upload files to the SAP Document Management Service and use them in SAP Build Process Automation processes?"
Although this is not impossible, there was a problem with uploading files from SAP Build Apps to DMS. (There is also a method using JavaScript, but it is not suitable as a NoCode tool.)

I thought of a way to upload without code, and wrote this article as a preparatory explanation, but in the meantime, a new feature was added to SAP Build Apps, and it has become much easier, so I would like to explain it.

Previous explanation: About the file attachment function of SAP Build Process Automation

When you use the file attachment feature in SAP Build Process Automation, the file is saved in SAP Document Management Service. Have you ever seen how files are stored on the SAP Document Management Service side? Actually, a folder is created for each file, and the files are stored in that folder. SAP Document Management Service assigns a unique ID to each folder and file for management purposes. In SAP Build Process Automation, attached files are handled using the (automatically created) folder ID rather than the file ID. This is correct

 

 

spa-res:cmis:folderid:<folder ID on DMS>

 

 

It is handled in this format.

In other words, if you upload a file to SAP Document Management Service with SAP Build Apps and want to use it in the workflow of SAP Build Process Automation, on the Build Apps side

  1. Create a folder on the SAP Document Management Service repository. At that time, obtain the ID issued to the folder.
  2. Upload the file to the above folder.
  3. When starting the SApBuild Process Automation workflow from SAP Build Apps, pass the file save destination information in the format "spa-res:cmis:folderid:<folder ID on DMS>"

That is necessary.

Preparation: Create a destination

Create a Destination to access SAP Document Management Service from SAP Build Apps.I will create this by cloning the Destination "sap_process_automation_document_store" in order to link SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Document Management Service. I thought it would be a good idea to set additional properties for use with SAP Build Apps in "sap_process_automation_document_store", but this is a Destination that has a specified method for collaboration and is automatically created. Therefore, although the content is almost the same, we will create another one for use from SAP Build Apps.

I cloned "sap_process_automation_document_store" and created a Destination called "sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps".
This Destination has the following changes from "sap_process_automation_document_store".
URL: Add /root to the end
Furthermore, the following is added as Additional Properties.

BuildApps.ApiTyperest
BuildApps.Enabledtrue
HTML5.DynamicDestinationtrue
MobileEnabledtrue

Honestly, I don't think adding "/root" to the URL is necessary. However, when I checked the API used by Document Management Service in SAP Business Accelerator Hub, I decided that the URL with "/root" added could be used commonly, so I added it to reduce the effort. The items set in Additional Properties are the settings for using this Destination with SAP Build Apps.

 

I'll explain this while creating a simple app.

 

1. Creating a process on the SAP Build Process Automation side

First, create a workflow that will receive attachment information. Since this is for operation verification, I will create a very simple one.

The API trigger is used as trigger, and It will link from SAP Build Apps using the SAP Build Library .
Set "FolderId" as an input variable for the process.

Set a variable of type "DocumentFolder".

The form connected from the trigger is also simple.

Place only file objects. Please check the ReadOnly attribute for this file object.
Assign the "FolderId" set as an input variable to this form on the process.

Set the form Subject, Recipients User, etc. appropriately. For testing, let's set the Recipients User to be ourselves. Recipients User specifies "Process started by" in Process metadata.
Release and deploy. Remember to run Publish from the lobby so it is available from SAP Build Apps.

 

This is going to be long, so I'll continue with Part 2.

entry.title: How to upload files to DMS using SAP Build Apps and use them in SAP Build Process Automation:Part2
entry.content:
*This article is a continuation of Part 1.

2. Creating SAP Build Apps side

First, set two variables as Application Variables.

FolderId and FolderName. Create these two in text type. There is no problem with using Page Variable instead of Application Variable, so please use the appropriate one according to the actual purpose.

SAP Build Apps has prepared a simple screen like this.

 

There are only two buttons. You can probably guess what the button does from the contents of its label, but let's create the logic for each button.

Before that, let's install the new features of SAP Build Apps explained in Part 1.
Go to MARKET PLACE and search for "HTTP destination"

Install the module called "HTTP destination request". Follow the same steps to also search for "Pick Files" and install the "Pick Files" component as well.

3.Creating the logic for the Upload File button

Now let's create the logic for the Upload File button.
As explained in Part 1, in order to use files uploaded to DMS with SAP Build Process Automation, it is necessary to create a folder and upload the files there. So this button

  1. Select files to upload
  2. Create a folder in DMS (& get folder ID)
  3. Upload the file to the folder created in above

We will write the logic. The entire logic is below.

It would be too long to explain everything, so I will only explain the parts that I think are important.

1. Let the Pick Files control select files.
Here, please change "Pick multiple files?" in OPTIONAL INPUTS to False so that only one file can be selected.

2. Create(Generate) a folder name on DMS and set it to variable: FolderName.

For Assigned value, use Formula and set it as follows this time.

Make the folder name the timestamp value with the preposition "FromBuildApps-". I think this will probably be unique.

3. Create a folder on DMS using an HTTP destination request.
Create a folder on the DMS using an HTTP destination request.

For Destination, specify "sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps" created in Part 1, for HTTP Method, specify POST, and for Request body type, specify multipart/form-data.
Specify the Request body using "List of values" as shown in the table below. If there is no cautionary note, set it as Static Text.

keyvalue
cmisactioncreateFolder
propertyId[0]cmis:name
propertyValue[0]Select application variables : FolderName
propertyId[1]cmis:objectTypeId
propertyValue[1]cmis:folder
succincttrue

４．Obtain the FolderId value from the return value of the folder creation API.
If the folder is created on DMS in Step 3 (if successful), the following value will be returned from the API.

 

 

{
  "succinctProperties":
    {
      "cmis:objectId": "fWtCFDH4ah2BGeFqzoorOLETNNq65cmp82KfYUSpWxA",
      "sap:parentIds": ["5daf1b700094ead661934376"],
      "cmis:name": "FromBuildApps-1737086879955",
      "cmis:lastModifiedBy": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:objectTypeId": "cmis:folder",
      "cmis:lastModificationDate": 1737086882353,
      "cmis:createdBy": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:baseTypeId": "cmis:folder",
      "cmis:parentId": "5daf1b700094ead661934376",
      "sap:owner": "sb-ab7437c3-137b-4c58-ac6f-a0fe26232136!b141614|sdm-di-sdm_di_prod-prod!b41064",
      "cmis:creationDate": 1737086882353,
      "cmis:changeToken": "0",
    },
  "exactACL": false,
}

 

 

The ObjectId of the folder that must be sent to the SAP Build Process Automation process is the value of "cmis:objectId". Assign this value to the variable FolderId.
This uses Formula,

 

STRING(<Output of HTTP destination request to create folder>.resBodyParsed.succinctProperties["cmis:objectId"])

 

You can obtain it by specifying . "<Output of HTTP destination request to create folder>.resBodyParsed" is correct if you select it from output of another node in Variables displayed on the left.

5. Upload files to DMS
Similar to Step 3, this uses the HTTP destination request to operate the DMS file upload API.
For Destination, specify "sap_process_automation_document_store_ForApps" created in Part 1, for HTTP Method, specify "POST", and for Request body type, specify "multipart/form-data".

Specify the Request body using "List of values" as shown in the table below. If there is no cautionary note, set it as Static Text.

keyvalue
cmisactioncreateDocument
propertyId[0]cmis:name
propertyValue[0]

Use Formula:
Specify files[0].name of PickFiles - which created step1.

propertyId[1]cmis:objectTypeId
propertyValue[1]cmis:document
filenamesame as PropertyValue[0]
_charset_UTF-8
includeAllowableActionstrue
succinctfalse
mediaUse Formula:
Specify files[0] of PickFiles - which created step1.

An important point to note is that in DMS, when uploading files to a folder, you must specify the path. This is a form in which the path name (folder name) is included in the API endpoint. Therefore, open the OPTIONAL INPUTS of the HTTP destination request and use the formula for Path to use the folder name added in the form "/"+appVars.FolderName.

OK, It will be able to upload to DMS.

The logic for the File Upload button is now complete. Next, we will develop the logic for the Execute Workflow button that will link to the SAP Build Process Automation process with the information of this uploaded file.

This is getting too long, so I'll continue with Part 3.

entry.title: How to upload files to DMS using SAP Build Apps and use them in SAP Build Process Automation:Part3
entry.content:
*This article is a continuation of Part 2. Here is part 1.

4.Creating the logic for the Execute Workflow button
